name: penumbra-glsl-00-sphere
description: CPig live preview through the IN-PROCESS GLSL studio (Swath C) â€” the STRUCTURAL (tape) render path + a shape POSITIVE CONTROL. Loads 54_penumbra_sphere.json via the Slop loader (a single Field Primitive sphere â†’ CPig Penumbra Preview), sets the preview backend to GLSL, Build â†’ CPig pushes a .3mf whose libfive primitive exports STRUCTURALLY (a compiled tape, not a voxel-bake) and renders in the in-process GLSL conduit. Pairs with penumbra-glsl-01-cpig-gyroid as a discrimination test: this one FAILS if the render is a lattice/blank (it must be a smooth sphere); the gyroid one FAILS if the render is a sphere (bug 0054). Together they prove the GLSL conduit renders the actual field shape on BOTH paths (tape here, dense grid there). PREVIEW DISCIPLINE: the Slop fixture sets preview:false on all field nodes so ONLY the Penumbra render appears in the viewport. ASSERTS: deterministic panel asserts (SlopSuccess / Connected / Status=Pushed / no FATAL). VISUAL: mode:capture â€” saves the candidate for manual review. VLM regression is wired but dormant (setup.vlm = ollama qwen2.5vl:7b + a sphere-vs-lattice vlmDescription) â€” re-enable by flipping the checkpoint mode back to vlm. Requires the Penumbra.Rhino plug-in auto-loaded + the CPig .gha (Penumbra Preview, GUID â€¦BA200).

setup.vlmDescription: A 3D ray-marched SDF surface drawn into the Rhino viewport: a single SMOOTH SPHERE â€” one solid round ball with a continuous, unbroken, shaded surface. The defining feature is one smooth closed spherical surface. It must NOT be a lattice; NOT a porous, maze-like, or gyroid structure; NOT have holes, passages, or repeating cells; NOT be an empty grey viewport.
